note: n=3 opening pairs (6 games per weight set); strength conclusions are explicitly out of scope for this stage. 60-game full run is a separate, user-approved follow-up.

t125ReuseAssessment.reason: gitCommit and evalCliSha256 (engine binary) differ from T125's run despite identical opening set, protocol parameters, and Edax config; 10 engine/ commits landed between T125 and this task's HEAD, so the search/eval binary itself is not the same artifact. Per task requirement, both candidate and v4 baseline were played fresh in this pilot.

additions: --opening-limit N: restricts match play to the first N openings of the selected --opening-set (default None = unrestricted, preserves prior behavior); recorded in settings/run_key as opening_limit
per-game wallClockSec field (time.monotonic() around play_game()) plus progress-line [X.Xs] suffix, for 60-game duration estimation

games.note: exactFallback=true at empties=22 (budgeted exact-quota attempt did not finish within budget; documented protocol behavior, not an error)
exactFallback=true at empties=22 (documented protocol behavior, not an error)

pairDeltas.interpretation: n=3 pairs only; direction (candidate margin better on average) is reported as raw fact, not as a strength conclusion. Sign is mixed across pairs (one pair -8) and variance is high.

watchPointEmpties19.description: Qualitative check for suspicious disc-diff crashes or blunders by the candidate around the empties~19-22 budgeted-to-exact transition, across all 12 games
watchPointEmpties19.finding: No suspicious blunder/disc-diff crash attributable to the candidate specifically was found. discDiff stabilizes to the final margin cleanly once unlimited-exact mode engages at empties<=20 in all 12 games. The largest budgeted-to-exact swing was on the candidate side (primary-02/black, -16.83 at empties=22 to -38 at empties=20), but v4 lost the same opening/color pairing even more heavily (-44), suggesting the opening itself is unfavorable rather than a candidate-specific defect. v4 showed exactFallback=true (budgeted exact-quota attempt not completed within budget) in 2 of 6 games at empties=22; candidate showed 0 of 6. This is a documented protocol behavior (exactQuotaPercent fallback), not an anomaly, but the frequency difference should be re-checked at 60-game scale.
watchPointEmpties19.followUp: Re-examine this watch-point at full 60-game scale if/when that run is approved.

resumeTest.description: Interrupt-and-resume test performed separately from the main 6+6 pilot games, using the candidate weights and a single opening (2 games planned), output written to the session scratchpad (not committed to the repo).
steps: Launched detached process via PowerShell Start-Process with --opening-limit 1 (2 games planned)
Polled log until game 1/2 completed (opening=primary-01, engineIsBlack=true, margin=-18)
Forcibly killed the process 3s into game 2 via Stop-Process -Force
Verified results.json persisted exactly 1 completed game (game_id=1, margin=-18) at that point

Re-ran the identical command (no --no-resume); log showed '[resume] loaded 1 already-completed game(s)', fixed-depth/node-budget regressions skipped as already completed, and only game 2 (game_id=2, opening=primary-01, engineIsBlack=false) was newly played
Final results.json contained both games with game_id=1 unchanged (margin=-18) and game_id=2 newly added (margin=-18); no duplication or loss
resumeTest.result: PASSED — atomic per-game checkpoint and resume confirmed working end-to-end on an actual interruption

sixtyGameEstimate.basis: pilot per-game wallClockSec (n=6 per weight set) plus measured fixed overhead per invocation
sixtyGameEstimate.recommendedTimeBudgetMinutes: 30-45 (safety margin; pilot per-game time varied ~2x, 11.9s-24.8s, and untested openings among the remaining 27 may include slower empties-22..28 combinatorics)
sixtyGameEstimate.concurrencyWarning: Both runs use a wall-clock (--engine-time-ms 1500) budget for the engine's move selection during the budgeted phase; running candidate and v4 processes concurrently would cause CPU contention that reduces actual search depth within the same wall-clock budget, silently altering results and breaking comparability with a protocol-identical run. Sequential execution (as done in this pilot) must be preserved for the 60-game run.
sixtyGameEstimate.t125ReuseNotApplicable: v4 side requires a fresh 60-game run too (see t125ReuseAssessment); it cannot be skipped.

verdict: none (information gathering only, per task scope)
outOfScope: placing any weight file under train/weights/
